entry.title: her name
entry.content:
the original md text:

currently i am using stash to manage my pornography collections.

there are some quirky shit in this wonderful horny FOSS app, and here are some tweaks:

  • no stable connection to many JAV db sites unless you set a local proxy in the "config.yml" manunally when using it inside the GFW
  • no autocreation of performers' names, you have to creat name columns for your ladies by your own hand
  • anime db is lacking, likely
  • DLNA is not working properly on my win PC idk may check it later
  • not very consistant when playing videos in a clean and focus 'new tab'
  • watching history is not fully recorded, or does it?

anyway, the ladies now have names thanks to nyaa github aria2 python etc etc

the prompt: you are a assitant for writting and editing blog articles in english, check the grammar and spelling, and put more details in sentences, make it more fluent and cohesive, i will send the aritles in ""

the answer from deepseek:

nothing, it fucking killed itself after generating something maybe because "GFW" or "proxy" is mentioned in the aritcle

the claude version:

Currently, I'm using Stash to manage my adult content collection. While this open-source application is wonderfully feature-rich for organizing adult media, I've encountered several quirks and limitations that required some workarounds.

Connection and Database Issues

Connectivity Problems with JAV Databases
One significant challenge is the unstable connection to many Japanese Adult Video (JAV) database sites when accessing Stash from behind the Great Firewall. The only solution I've found is to manually configure a local proxy in the config.yml file, which isn't the most user-friendly approach but gets the job done.

Manual Performer Management
Unlike some other media management tools, Stash doesn't automatically create performer profiles. This means I have to manually create name entries for each performer in my collection, which can be time-consuming when organizing a large library.

Limited Anime Database Support
The anime-related database integration appears to be lacking or incomplete. This is particularly noticeable if your collection includes animated adult content, as metadata retrieval and organization options seem limited in this category.

Technical Glitches

DLNA Functionality
DLNA streaming isn't working properly on my Windows PC. I'm not sure if this is a configuration issue on my end or a broader compatibility problem, but it's something I plan to investigate further.

Inconsistent Video Playback
The video player experience isn't always consistent, especially when attempting to open videos in a clean, focused new tab. The interface sometimes behaves unpredictably in this viewing mode.

Viewing History Tracking
I'm uncertain whether the watch history feature is functioning correctly. It seems like my viewing records aren't being fully captured or stored consistently—though I'm still determining if this is a bug or a misunderstanding of how the feature works.

The Silver Lining

Despite these challenges, I've successfully organized my collection and properly tagged all performers. This achievement was made possible through a combination of tools and resources, including Nyaa, GitHub repositories, aria2 for downloading, Python scripts for automation, and various other utilities. Now my collection is properly cataloged, and all the performers have their names correctly assigned.

the chatGPT version:

Currently, I am using Stash to manage my personal pornography collection.

Overall, it is a fantastic and impressively capable FOSS application, but it does come with a few quirks that are worth mentioning. Below are some observations and minor tweaks based on my experience:

  • Unstable access to JAV databases behind the GFW. When running Stash inside the Great Firewall, connections to many Japanese adult video database sites are unreliable unless you manually configure a local proxy in config.yml. This setup is not particularly beginner-friendly and requires hands-on configuration.

  • No automatic performer name creation. Performer metadata is not generated automatically. You must manually create and manage name fields for performers, which can be tedious if you are dealing with a large collection.

  • Weak anime database support. Anime-related metadata is noticeably lacking, likely due to limited upstream database coverage.

  • DLNA issues on Windows. DLNA streaming does not work reliably on my Windows PC. This might be a configuration issue on my end, and I plan to investigate it further later.

  • Inconsistent playback behavior. Video playback in a clean, distraction-free “new tab” mode is not always consistent, which can break the viewing experience.

  • Incomplete viewing history tracking. Watching history does not appear to be fully or reliably recorded—although it is unclear whether this is a bug or simply a limitation of the current implementation.

That said, the most important issue has now been resolved: the performers finally have proper names. Thanks to a combination of tools and resources—such as nyaa, GitHub projects, aria2, and some Python scripting—the metadata situation has improved significantly.

Despite its rough edges, Stash remains a powerful and flexible solution, especially for users who are comfortable tweaking and extending their setup.

the human comment:

they definitely write better than the human, unfortunately they can not keep the humourus tone in a humanoid way

第四十二章

道生一①，一生二②，二生三③，三生万物。万物负阴而抱阳④，冲气以为和⑤。人之所恶，唯孤、寡、不谷⑥，而王公以为称。故物或损之而益，或益之而损。人之所教，我亦教之。强梁者不得其死，吾将以为教父⑦。

第四十二章

道は一を生み、一は二を生み、二は三を生み、三は万物を生む。万物は陰を負い、陽を抱き、衝気をもって和を成す。人が嫌うのは、孤独、少数、不作であり、王公はこれを称とする。故に、物には損をして益となるものもあれば、益をして損となるものもある。人が教えることは、私もまた教える。強い者はその死を得ず、私はこれを教父と見る。

entry.title: First Blade
entry.content:
First Blade

In the distant future, humanity had expanded beyond Earth, establishing colonies across the stars. Among them was Aeloria, a lush planet that thrived under three suns, known for its striking landscapes and immense resources. However, it was also known for the tension brewing between its inhabitants: the valiant Aelorans and the oppressive Syndicate ruling from the shadows.

At the heart of this conflict was Lyra Voss, a skilled engineer whose parents had been lost to the Syndicate’s relentless pursuit of power. Raised in the shadow of the sprawling metropolis of Nexis, she had spent her life dreaming of rebellion. The Syndicate's enforcers, known as the “Blades,” were feared throughout the galaxies for their ruthless efficiency.

One fateful night, while scavenging through the ruins of an ancient temple, Lyra discovered a forgotten artifact — the First Blade, a legendary weapon said to possess the power of a thousand stars. The blade shimmered with an ethereal light, inscribed with markings that no one understood. With a sense of destiny awakening within her, Lyra took it, unaware of the dormant power it held.

Empowered by the blade, Lyra joined the Rising Dawn, a group of rebels committed to overthrowing the Syndicate’s reign. Their leader, Kael Varis, was war-hardened and charismatic, with a past that haunted him. Despite his reservations about entrusting such a powerful weapon to a young engineer, he saw the spark in Lyra — a reflection of his own fierce determination.

As the Rising Dawn planned their first major strike against the Syndicate, whispers of Lyra’s blade spread. Some viewed it as a beacon of hope, while others considered it a threat too great to be allowed to exist. The Syndicate dispatched their most skilled Blade, Xander Kaelith, who was known for his ruthless tactics and unyielding loyalty. He had heard the legends of the First Blade and was determined to claim it for the Syndicate.

Navigating treachery and betrayal, Lyra's journey took her across Aeloria’s stunning yet deadly terrain, from crystalline forests to labyrinthine caves. The more she trained with the blade, the stronger her connection grew. Voices whispered in her mind, revealing secrets of the universe and the energy that connected all life.

As the day of reckoning approached, the Rising Dawn launched their assault on Syndicate headquarters, a towering fortress that loomed over Nexis. In the chaotic clash of blades and energy weapons, Lyra confronted Xander, her one-time ally turned adversary.

In a climactic duel, the power of the First Blade erupted, illuminating the battlefield. As Lyra and Xander danced between light and shadow, she discovered that true strength lay not in the weapon but in the unity of her people. With the blade's energy, she ignited the hearts of the oppressed, turning the tide of battle.

In a moment of clarity, Lyra offered Xander a choice — to join the fight for freedom or perish in his ambition. As dawn broke over Aeloria, it signaled not just a new day but a revolution fueled by hope. The First Blade had not merely been a weapon; it was the symbol of a new beginning, uniting the divided and forging a path towards a brighter future among the stars.

entry.title: the second blade
entry.content:
The Witch and the Necromancer

In the heart of the Eldenwood Forest, under a moonlit sky, a witch named Elara was weaving spells among the ancient oaks. Her powers, drawn from the earth and moon, kept the balance of life in the realm. Despite her enchanting gifts, whispers of a necromancer named Kael plagued the village nearby, sowing fear and curiosity.

Kael, a once-revered healer turned dark sorcerer, delved into forbidden arts, resurrecting the dead at a cost—a life for a life. The villagers trembled as his dark magic summoned spirits to do his bidding. But Elara, understanding the tether between life and death, believed he could be redeemed.

During a night thick with fog, Elara ventured to Kael's lair, a crumbling tower entwined with gnarled roots. The air crackled with tension as she stepped inside, drawn by the wails of restless souls. Kael, cloaked in shadows, met her gaze with eyes like smoldering coals.

"Why do you disturb my work, witch?" he hissed.

"I come not to confront, but to understand," Elara replied, her voice steady. “You have the power to mend, not just to break.”

Kael laughed, but the sound was hollow. “And what do you know of loss? Of yearning for what is irretrievably gone?”

"You seek to control death, but life cannot thrive under pressure. Let me show you another way."

Intrigued, Kael hesitated, the pain in his heart visible for the first time. Elara offered a glimmer of hope, leading him away from the dark path he'd chosen.

Together, they wove a new spell, one that honored the dead while respecting the living. With the forest as their witness, they forged a bond that transformed shadows into light, a witch and a necromancer united—two sides of the same coin.
